steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $x^4(7x^2 - 2x + 9)$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Explanation
steps.content: Identify the greatest common factor (GCF) of all three terms: $7x^{6}$, $-2x^{5}$, and $9x^{4}$. The GCF of the coefficients 7, 2, and 9 is 1. The GCF of $x^{6}$, $x^{5}$, and $x^{4}$ is $x^{4}$. So GCF $= x^{4}$.

steps.title: Explanation
steps.content:
Evaluating all choices:
  • Choice A — ‘$x^5(7x - 2)$’: $x^{5}(7x - 2)$. Expanding gives $7x^{6} - 2x^{5}$, missing the $9x^{4}$ term. Incorrect.
  • Choice B — ‘$x^4(7x^2 - 2x + 9)$’: $x^{4}(7x^{2} - 2x + 9)$. Expanding gives $7x^{6} - 2x^{5} + 9x^{4}$ ✓. That confirms B is correct.
  • Choice C — ‘$x^4(7x^2 - 2x + 1)$’: $x^{4}(7x^{2} - 2x + 1)$. Expanding gives $7x^{6} - 2x^{5} + x^{4}$, not $9x^{4}$. Incorrect.
  • Choice D — ‘$x^6(-2x^5 + 9x^4 + 1)$’: $x^{6}(-2x^{5} + 9x^{4} + 1)$. Expanding gives terms with $x^{11}$ and $x^{10}$, completely different. Incorrect.
